emitted.condition: duplicate_acceptance_rate denominator > 0 and numerator == 0 for experienceos_local
emitted.text: Under the documented offline lifecycle configuration, duplicate memory proposals were accepted into active state in 0/2 eligible ExperienceOS local-policy cases, compared with 2/2 for append-only storage.

Across the eligible custom retrieval expectations, ExperienceOS rules selected 15/17 expected memories into context, compared with 0/17 for the stateless baseline, which has no accumulated experience.

emitted.condition: full-history reference exists with matching accounting
emitted.text: ExperienceOS rules supplied 33.7% fewer approximated comparable context tokens than full-history prompting (1166/3461 token reduction across 38 eligible custom cases, same accounting method).

emitted.condition: state-corruption denominator > 0; real_model_used false and disclosed
emitted.text: In the scripted local-policy cases, final lifecycle state diverged from the oracle in 7/38 executed cases (the numerator counts unfulfilled aspirational memory expectations as well); every scripted invalid proposal (duplicate, inactive-target, nonexistent-target, malformed) was rejected or contained by the engine. This is the scripted-plus-fallback offline mode and does not measure real-GGUF proposal accuracy.

emitted.condition: external selection denominators > 0; subset and proxy scope stated
emitted.text: In the LongMemEval 50-case stratified subset (structural offline run, official data, no official judge), ExperienceOS rules selected the official answer-bearing session in 14/50 cases — behind naive lexical retrieval at 42/50 — while supplying orders of magnitude fewer context tokens than full history. Sparse rule-based extraction on conversational data is a measured limitation.
In the LongMemEval 50-case stratified subset, ExperienceOS rules supplied 99.8% fewer approximated context tokens than full-history prompting across 50 cases (same accounting method; structural run).

withheld.reason: stale rendered-context leakage is 10/11 for ExperienceOS rules — the dataset's aspirational unkeyed-domain update oracles are honestly failed, so no exclusion claim is emitted

forgotten-content exclusion is 0/2 on the eligible post-forget answers (one case is a missed paraphrased forget; the other is the restatement scenario where the term legitimately reappears) — no blanket exclusion claim is emitted

display_label_mappings.answer_context_presence_rate: Verbatim evidence-turn content present in supplied context (exact substring; normalized ExperienceOS memory text cannot match verbatim by construction)
display_label_mappings.forgotten_exclusion_rate: Forgotten-content exclusion (post-forget answers; the restatement scenario re-mentions the term legitimately and is counted against exclusion by construction)
display_label_mappings.local_state_corruption_rate: Final-state divergence from oracle (includes never-created aspirational memories, not only destroyed ones)
display_label_mappings.stale_context_leakage_rate: Stale content in rendered context (cases with an expected-superseded value)
